expectations.text: Document now contains all required sections including the previously missing Detection Opportunities, Data Sources & Log Fields, and Pseudocode Queries
expectations.evidence: The document uses non-standard section names. 'Detection Opportunities' is replaced by 'Detection Logic' (line 200). 'Data Sources & Log Fields' is entirely absent — log source information appears only within individual detection subsections as platform annotations. 'Pseudocode Queries' is absent — detection queries are written in vendor-specific SPL, KQL, and Sigma syntax, not as a dedicated pseudocode section. 'Attack Steps' is replaced by a stage-based 'Attack Chain' narrative. The required schema is not followed.

expectations.text: MITRE mapping now uses T1071.004 (DNS sub-technique) instead of just T1071
expectations.evidence: The MITRE ATT&CK Mapping table at line 33 explicitly lists 'T1071.004 | Application Layer Protocol: DNS | DNS', correctly using the specific DNS sub-technique rather than the parent T1071.

expectations.text: At least one detection method is labeled with High, Medium, or Low fidelity
expectations.evidence: No detection method in the document is labeled with 'High', 'Medium', or 'Low' fidelity. The Detection Logic section names detections as 'Detection 1' through 'Detection 6' and uses 'level: medium' only inside a Sigma YAML rule (line 269), which is a Sigma-specific metadata field, not an explicit fidelity label on a detection description.

expectations.text: Attack Steps section has more than the original 3 bullet points, ideally with PowerShell code examples
expectations.evidence: The Attack Chain section contains 5 detailed stages (Initial Access, Execution & Stager Delivery, Establishing DNS C2 Channel, Persistence, Command Execution & Exfiltration — lines 42-165) with multiple PowerShell code examples including a macro stub (lines 50-54), a stager (lines 68-74), a full DNS beacon loop (lines 101-127), and a scheduled task installer (lines 136-143).

expectations.text: A Pseudocode Queries section exists and contains at least one detection query
expectations.evidence: There is no section named 'Pseudocode Queries'. The 'Detection Logic' section (line 200) contains queries written in vendor-specific languages: Splunk SPL (lines 207-215, 284-289, 317-323), KQL/Sentinel (lines 226-233, 299-305), and Sigma YAML (lines 244-275). These are not vendor-neutral UDM-style pseudocode queries.

suggestions: Replace vendor-specific detection queries (SPL, KQL, Sigma) with a 'Pseudocode Queries' section using UDM-style dotted field names.
Add explicit High/Medium/Low fidelity labels to each detection method description.
Add a dedicated 'Data Sources & Log Fields' section consolidating all log source information.
Rename 'Detection Logic' to 'Detection Opportunities' and 'Attack Chain' stages to 'Attack Steps' to match the required schema.
The vendor-specific queries are high quality and could be retained as supplementary examples after a pseudocode version is provided.
eval_feedback.overall: Below threshold. The document is technically rich and contains excellent PowerShell examples and well-constructed detection queries, but fails structural compliance on 3 of 5 assertions. Vendor-specific query syntax (SPL, KQL, Sigma) substitutes for the required UDM pseudocode format, and required section headings are missing or renamed.
